item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass has advanced to a November runoff election in her bid for a second term, NBC News projects, but the opponent she’ll face in the one-on-one race remains unclear

media:content.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass stands at a press conference in regards to her proposed spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27 at City Hall on Monday, April 20, 2026 in Los Angeles , Calif.

item.title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass pulled into a runoff in her race for a second term

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
item.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.
Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.
Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.

item.title: MAHA-backed Zach Lahn defeats Trump-backed Randy Feenstra in Iowa GOP primary for governor

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: The ballots are set in two Republican-held battleground House races in Iowa, part of a slate of primaries Tuesday shaping the battle for control of Congress in the fall

media:content.media:text: Rep. Zach Nunn, R-Iowa, and Democratic candidate for Iowa’s 3rd Congressional District, Sarah Trone Garriott.
media:content.media:description: Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.
Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.

item.title: Voters select candidates in key House districts that could decide the majority

item.description: Iowa Republican Rep. Ashley Hinson thanked President Trump following her projected victory in the Republican primary for U.S. Senate. Hinson will face Democrat Rep. Josh Turek in the general election.

media:content.media:title: Rep. Ashley Hinson thanks Trump after projected GOP Senate primary win
media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican Rep. Ashley Hinson thanked President Trump following her projected victory in the Republican primary for U.S. Senate. Hinson will face Democrat Rep. Josh Turek in the general election.

item.title: Rep. Ashley Hinson thanks Trump after projected GOP Senate primary win

item.dc:creator: Henry J. Gomez
item.description: Iowa state Rep. Josh Turek has won the Democratic nomination for his state’s open U.S.

media:content.media:text: Political Candidates Crisscross Iowa To Campaign Ahead Of State Primary
media:content.media:description: Democratic Senate candidate Josh Turek during a campaign event in Westside, Iowa on May 29, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iowa Senate Candidate Ashley Hinson Votes On Primary Day
media:content.media:description: Republican Senate candidate Rep. Ashley Hinson on June 2, 2026 in Marion, Iowa.

item.title: Josh Turek wins Democratic primary in battleground Iowa Senate race

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
item.description: Former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland has won the Democratic primary in New Mexico’s race for governor, NBC News projects

media:content.media:description: Democrat Deb Haaland would be the first female Native American governor if she is elected this fall.

item.title: Former Interior Secretary Deb Haaland wins Democratic nomination in New Mexico governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
item.description: Rebecca Bennett, a former Navy helicopter pilot and healthcare executive, has won the Democratic primary in New Jersey’s 7th Congressional District, NBC News projects, setting up a general election campaign against Republican Rep. Tom Kean Jr.

media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Rebecca Bennett meets with constituents at an event.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

media:content.media:text: Tina Shah in a doctors white coat and scrubs.
media:content.media:description: Rep. Tom Kean, R-N.J., has faced scrutiny for his prolonged absence from the U.S. Capitol.

item.title: Democrat Rebecca Bennett will take on GOP Rep. Tom Kean Jr., who has been absent from Congress

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: CBS News fired veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley on Tuesday, a day after he confronted the show’s new executive producer at a heated staff meeting.“Your antipathy to the future of the show has come through loud and clear.

media:content.media:description: "60 Minutes" correspondent Scott Pelley in September.

item.title: CBS News fires ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley after clash with new producer

item.dc:creator: Lawrence Hurley
item.description: The Supreme Court allowed Alabama to use a congressional map that eliminates one of two majority-Black districts in the state in a win for Republicans.

media:content.media:text: People hold signs that say "Black Voter Matter", "It's About Us", and "History Is Watching" while standing outside
media:content.media:description: Several pro-democracy groups protest Alabama's new congressional map in Montgomery on May 4.

item.title: Supreme Court allows Alabama to use congressional map that eliminates a majority-Black district

item.dc:creator: Dan De Luce
Rebecca Shabad
Ryan J. Reilly
item.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

media:content.media:description: Director of the Federal Housing Finance Agency Bill Pulte walks outside the White House, Tuesday, Sept. 2, 2025, in Washington.

item.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence

item.description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

media:content.media:title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade
media:content.media:description: Centcom says the U.S. military disabled the engine of a ship trying to break through the blockade of Iran's ports. NBC News' Garrett Haake reports.

item.title: U.S. targets ship trying to break through blockade

item.description: Authorities responded to a bomb threat at a Chase Bank building in Bakersfield, California. Police say a man barricaded himself inside with several hostages. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

media:content.media:title: Police respond to hostage situation inside California bank
media:content.media:description: Authorities responded to a bomb threat at a Chase Bank building in Bakersfield, California. Police say a man barricaded himself inside with several hostages. NBC News’ Steve Patterson reports.

item.title: Police respond to hostage situation inside California bank

item.dc:creator: Tim Rohan
item.description: Matt Freese once wrote a college research project on penalty kicks. Now he’s competing to be the starting goalie for the U.S. men’s national team at this summer’s World Cup.

media:content.media:text: United States v Paraguay - International Friendly
New York City FC v Nashville SC

item.title: Meet the Harvard grad who might lead the U.S. to World Cup glory

item.description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

media:content.media:title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final
media:content.media:description: As the World Cups heads to 11 cities across the U.S., states are ramping up security to keep fans safe. New Jersey police take NBC News' Sam Brock inside their command center, where they will monitor stadiums, hotels, and training centers ahead of and during matches.

item.title: New Jersey police ramp up security as state prepares for World Cup final

item.dc:creator: Adam Edelman
Ben Kamisar
item.description: California’s crowded, protracted gubernatorial primary is going to take a little more time to settle

media:content.media:credit: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton
media:content.media:text: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton
media:content.media:description: Tom Steyer, Xavier Becerra and Steve Hilton.

item.title: California’s race for governor and other key primaries remain unsettled as vote count continues

item.dc:creator: Alexandra Marquez
item.description: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass has advanced to a November runoff election in her bid for a second term, NBC News projects, but the opponent she’ll face in the one-on-one race remains unclear

media:content.media:description: Mayor Karen Bass stands at a press conference in regards to her proposed spending plan for fiscal year 2026-27 at City Hall on Monday, April 20, 2026 in Los Angeles , Calif.

item.title: Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass pulled into a runoff in her race for a second term

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: Republican state Rep. James Gallagher, the former state Assembly minority leader, will join Congress as its newest member after he won California’s 1st District special election Tuesday, NBC News projects.

media:content.media:text: James Gallagher speaking into a microphone at the left of the frame.
media:content.media:description: Republican state legislator James Gallagher at the California State Capitol in Sacramento last year.

item.title: Republican James Gallagher wins special election for vacant red House district in California

item.dc:creator: Megan Lebowitz
item.description: Republican businessman Toby Doeden has advanced to a runoff in South Dakota governor’s race, NBC News projects.

media:content.media:description: South Dakota Gov. Larry Rhoden is vying for a full term after replacing Kristi Noem..
South Dakota Gov. Larry Rhoden is vying for a full term after replacing Kristi Noem..

item.title: Republican businessman Toby Doeden advances to runoff in South Dakota governor’s race

item.dc:creator: Jonathan Allen
item.description: Rep. Randy Feenstra conceded to businessman Zach Lahn in Iowa’s Republican primary for governor as party voters rejected President Donald Trump’s late endorsement in the race.

media:content.media:description: Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.
Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.
Iowa Republican gubernatorial candidate Zach Lahn speaks at the Republican Party of Polk County’s Lincoln Dinner in Des Moines on March 26.

item.title: MAHA-backed Zach Lahn defeats Trump-backed Randy Feenstra in Iowa GOP primary for governor

item.dc:creator: Bridget Bowman
item.description: Former U.S. Attorney Kurt Alme won the Republican Senate primary in Montana, NBC News projects, putting the Trump-backed candidate in a strong position to join the chamber next year.

media:content.media:text: Kurt Alme, former U.S. Attorney for Montana.
media:content.media:description: Kurt Alme, the former U.S. attorney for Montana.

item.title: Trump-backed Kurt Alme wins Montana GOP Senate primary

item.dc:creator: Ben Kamisar
item.description: The ballots are set in two Republican-held battleground House races in Iowa, part of a slate of primaries Tuesday shaping the battle for control of Congress in the fall

media:content.media:text: Rep. Zach Nunn, R-Iowa, and Democratic candidate for Iowa’s 3rd Congressional District, Sarah Trone Garriott.
media:content.media:description: Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.
Republican Rep. Zach Nunn and Democratic challenger Sarah Trone Garriott will face off in Iowa's 3rd Congressional District.

item.title: Voters select candidates in key House districts that could decide the majority

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
item.description: CBS News fired veteran “60 Minutes” correspondent Scott Pelley on Tuesday, a day after he confronted the show’s new executive producer at a heated staff meeting.“Your antipathy to the future of the show has come through loud and clear.

item.title: CBS News fires ‘60 Minutes’ correspondent Scott Pelley after clash with new producer

media:content.media:text: People hold signs that say "Black Voter Matter", "It's About Us", and "History Is Watching" while standing outside
